lede: A look at April 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Brenda Fruhvirtová and Thom Evans, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Liberation Day tariffs
events.desc: Liberation Day tariffs: U.S. President Donald Trump announces sweeping worldwide tariffs.

events.title: Garissa University College attack
events.desc: Gunmen attack Garissa University College in Kenya, killing at least 148 people and wounding 79 others.

events.title: Hatton Garden safe deposit burglary
events.desc: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area in what has been called the "largest burglary in English legal history."

events.title: 2012 Oikos University shooting
events.desc: A mass shooting at Oikos University in California leaves seven people dead and three injured.

events.title: India national cricket team
events.desc: India wins the Cricket World Cup for the second time in history under the captaincy of MS Dhoni.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev arrives in Havana, Cuba, to meet with Fidel Castro in an attempt to mend strained relations.

events.title: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 165 (1969)
events.desc: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 165 crashes into the Polica mountain near Zawoja, Poland, killing 53.

events.title: Australian Bureau of Statistics
events.desc: The Australian Bureau of Statistics conducts the country's first national census.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The largest in a series of Southern bread riots occurs in Richmond, Virginia.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: In the Battle of Copenhagen a British Royal Navy squadron defeats a hastily assembled, smaller, mostly-volunteer Dano-Norwegian Navy at high cost, forcing Denmark out of the Second League of Armed Neutrality.

births.name: Tayfun Korkut
births.line: Turkish football manager and former player

births.name: John Larsson
births.line: Swedish 17th General of The Salvation Army (died 2022)

births.name: Jack Webb
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter (died 1982)

births.name: Dominic Savio
births.line: Italian Catholic saint, adolescent student of Saint John Bosco (died 1857)

deaths.text: Khamtai Siphandone, Laotian politician, 4th President of Laos
John Argyris, Greek computer scientist, engineer, and academic
Toshitsugu Takamatsu, Japanese martial artist and educator
Ahmed Vefik Pasha, Greek playwright and politician, 249th Grand Vizier of the Ottoman Empire

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bronach of Glen-Seichis (Irish martyrology)
Christian feast day: Henry Budd (Anglican Church of Canada)
